prompt: Write one word in each gap.
explanation: Each pair of gaps tests a fixed adjective/verb + preposition pattern from this unit: famous for, look at, arrive at, provide somebody with, prepare for, suitable for, far from, close to, wait for, ask somebody about, late for, ask for.
passageTemplate:
1. Sydney is famous {{blank-1}} its harbour. You should also look {{blank-2}} the Opera House and the bridge while you're there.
2. We arrived {{blank-3}} the hotel and they provided us {{blank-4}} a map of the area.
3. When you're preparing {{blank-5}} a holiday, pack clothes that are suitable {{blank-6}} the place where you're going.
4. I prefer to be far {{blank-7}} other people when I'm on holiday. I don't like being close {{blank-8}} crowds of tourists.
5. While we were waiting {{blank-9}} our train, I asked someone {{blank-10}} the delay.
6. Will was late {{blank-11}} his appointment, so he asked me {{blank-12}} some money for a taxi.
